insertion_lines: # Verify MCP server was actually registered (claude mcp add exits 0 even on failure)
if ! claude mcp list 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^cctx-memory:'; then
echo "  ERROR: MCP server registration failed."
echo "  Check: claude mcp list — if empty, ~/.claude/ may be read-only or Claude Code outdated."
exit 1
fi
description: Detects when claude mcp add exits 0 without actually registering the MCP server.
rationale: Claude Code's mcp add command returns exit code 0 even when registration fails due to read-only ~/.claude/ directories, outdated Claude Code versions, or project-scope conflicts. This guard verifies the server appears in mcp list before declaring success.
expected_impact: Users with read-only ~/.claude/ or outdated Claude Code get an actionable error at install-time instead of discovering broken MCP tools later, eliminating one retry cycle and shortening time-to-working-memory by 10-15 minutes.
unified_diff:
--- a/installer/setup.sh
+++ b/installer/setup.sh
@@ -101,6 +101,13 @@
 claude mcp add -s user --transport http \
   cctx-memory "$CCTX_URL/mcp/" \
   --header "Authorization: Bearer $CCTX_TOKEN"
+
+# Verify MCP server was actually registered (claude mcp add exits 0 even on failure)
+if ! claude mcp list 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^cctx-memory:'; then
+  echo "  ERROR: MCP server registration failed."
+  echo "  Check: claude mcp list — if empty, ~/.claude/ may be read-only or Claude Code outdated."
+  exit 1
+fi
 
 echo "  Added MCP server: cctx-memory (user scope)"

score_reasons.guard_pattern_fidelity: Exactly matches canonical verify-mcp-registration pattern: comment, boolean check, two-line ERROR, exit 1, 7 lines total.
score_reasons.time_to_value: Detects F4 misconfig at install-time with actionable diagnostic, eliminating one retry cycle for read-only directories or outdated Claude Code.
score_reasons.failure_mode_coverage: Cites F4 and handles primary triggers (read-only directories, outdated Claude Code) but may miss project-scope conflict edge case.
score_reasons.error_craftsmanship: Two-line ERROR with two-space indent, names exact failed check and exact next command, zero marketing language, matches existing style.

insertion_lines: # Verify python3 is available (required for JSON parsing and hook configuration)
if ! command -v python3 &> /dev/null; then
echo "  ERROR: python3 not found but required for setup."
echo "  Check: command -v python3 — if missing, install python3 for your OS"
exit 1
fi
description: Detects missing python3 before JSON parsing fails with misleading API error.
rationale: The installer uses python3 in steps 2, 5, and hooks but never verifies it exists. Missing python3 causes step 2 to fail with "API responded but didn't return valid stats JSON" (wrong diagnosis) or step 5 to fail mid-execution. This guard catches the missing dependency at step 1 with an actionable fix.
error_message: ERROR: python3 not found but required for setup.
expected_impact: Users on minimal Docker images, fresh WSL, or macOS with removed system Python get a clear diagnostic in <1s instead of debugging misleading API errors or hook installation failures for 5-10 minutes.
unified_diff:
--- a/installer/setup.sh
+++ b/installer/setup.sh
@@ -65,6 +65,13 @@
 fi
 CLAUDE_VERSION=$(claude --version 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
 echo "  Found: claude $CLAUDE_VERSION"
+
+# Verify python3 is available (required for JSON parsing and hook configuration)
+if ! command -v python3 &> /dev/null; then
+  echo "  ERROR: python3 not found but required for setup."
+  echo "  Check: command -v python3 — if missing, install python3 for your OS"
+  exit 1
+fi
 
 # --- Step 2: Test API connection ---
 echo "[2/6] Testing API connection..."

score_reasons.guard_pattern_fidelity: Clean boolean check using existing command -v idiom, 7 lines, two-space ERROR format matches canonical pattern exactly.
score_reasons.time_to_value: Eliminates retry cycle by catching missing python3 at step 1 instead of misleading API error in step 2.
score_reasons.failure_mode_coverage: Introduces valid new mode with reproducible symptom (misleading API error) and clean single-boolean check, though edge-case.
score_reasons.error_craftsmanship: Two-line ERROR with diagnostic command, matches canonical style; fix suggestion necessarily generic due to platform differences.

anchor_line: claude mcp remove -s user cctx-memory 2>/dev/null || true
insertion_lines: # Verify removal succeeded (project-scope entry would still conflict)
if claude mcp list 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^cctx-memory:'; then
echo "  ERROR: Failed to remove existing MCP server 'cctx-memory'."
echo "  Check: claude mcp list — if project-scope, remove it manually."
exit 1
fi
description: Detects when claude mcp remove fails to clear a project-scope MCP server entry before re-adding.
rationale: The script only removes from user scope, so a project-scope cctx-memory entry survives and causes duplicate registrations or undefined behavior. This guard catches the conflict at install-time.
error_message: ERROR: Failed to remove existing MCP server 'cctx-memory'.
expected_impact: Users with pre-existing project-scope MCP entries get an actionable diagnostic instead of silent dual-registration or confusing tool-not-found errors later.
unified_diff:
--- a/installer/setup.sh
+++ b/installer/setup.sh
@@ -96,6 +96,12 @@
 if [ "$EXISTING" -gt 0 ]; then
   echo "  MCP server 'cctx-memory' already configured — removing old entry"
   claude mcp remove -s user cctx-memory 2>/dev/null || true
+  # Verify removal succeeded (project-scope entry would still conflict)
+  if claude mcp list 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^cctx-memory:'; then
+    echo "  ERROR: Failed to remove existing MCP server 'cctx-memory'."
+    echo "  Check: claude mcp list — if project-scope, remove it manually."
+    exit 1
+  fi
 fi
 
 claude mcp add -s user --transport http \

score_reasons.guard_pattern_fidelity: Clean boolean check, two-line ERROR, exit 1, insertion-only, reuses grep pattern; comment slightly less precise than canonical.
score_reasons.time_to_value: Detects project-scope MCP conflict at install-time with actionable diagnostic, eliminating one retry cycle for this F4 variant.
score_reasons.failure_mode_coverage: Cites F4 and handles project-scope conflict trigger but not other F4 variants like read-only directories or outdated Claude Code.
score_reasons.error_craftsmanship: Two-line error names what broke plus diagnostic command, universally applicable, matches existing two-space indent style exactly.

insertion_lines: # Verify downloaded content is actually a shell script (not captive portal HTML)
if ! head -1 "$TARGET" 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^#!/bin/bash'; then
echo "  ERROR: Hook script corrupted — check if you're behind a captive portal."
echo "  Check: head -5 $TARGET"
exit 1
fi
description: Detects when hook download succeeds but writes HTML instead of shell script due to captive portal interception.
rationale: Curl returns 200 OK behind captive portals but writes HTML login pages instead of bash scripts. The corrupted hook files would fail with cryptic syntax errors at runtime. This guard catches the substitution at install-time by verifying the shebang.
error_message: ERROR: Hook script corrupted — check if you're behind a captive portal.
expected_impact: Users behind captive portals or corporate proxies that intercept GitHub downloads get an immediate install-time diagnostic instead of cryptic runtime syntax errors. Eliminates one debug cycle.
unified_diff:
--- a/installer/setup.sh
+++ b/installer/setup.sh
@@ -132,6 +132,13 @@
     }
   fi
   chmod +x "$TARGET"
+
+  # Verify downloaded content is actually a shell script (not captive portal HTML)
+  if ! head -1 "$TARGET" 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^#!/bin/bash'; then
+    echo "  ERROR: Hook script corrupted — check if you're behind a captive portal."
+    echo "  Check: head -5 $TARGET"
+    exit 1
+  fi
 done
 
 # --- Step 5: Configure hooks in settings.json ---

score_reasons.guard_pattern_fidelity: Matches canonical verify-mcp-registration shape exactly: comment, boolean check, two-line ERROR, exit 1, seven lines, reuses grep -q.
score_reasons.time_to_value: Detects captive-portal hook corruption at install-time with actionable diagnostic, eliminating cryptic runtime bash errors and one full debug cycle.
score_reasons.failure_mode_coverage: Introduces valid new failure mode with specific trigger and reproducible symptom; handles captive portal HTML plus any non-bash corruption.
score_reasons.error_craftsmanship: Matches canonical pattern: two-space ERROR prefix, names what broke, universally-applicable diagnostic command, zero marketing language, terse and clear.
